revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Template journal/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Template journal/doc]]</includeonly>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Template journal/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Template journal/doc]]</includeonly>

#redirect[[:en:Template:Template journal/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Template journal/doc]]</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} {{Template shortcut|tj|TJ}} {{lua|Module:Params}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Template journal/styles.css}} The {{tl|Template journal}} template allows to showcase with colors and multiple lines the syntax of any template, while providing an easy way to display placeholder texts using colons as separators. For an identical template that displays the output in horizontal format, see {{tl|Template journal inline}}. See also {{tl|Template journal parameter}} for a template similar to {{tl|para}} that uses the same syntax and produces the same output as the parameters displayed by {{tl|tj}} and {{tl|tji}}. == Usage == ;Syntax :{{tji|tj|template|param. name 1: param. content 1|param. name 2: param. content 2...}} {{vpad}} For instance, the following code {{tj|cite book |last: Author's last name|first: Author's first name |author-link: Author's page name on Wikipedia|date: Publication date |title: Work's title|url: Work's URL|location: Where the work was published |publisher: Name of the publisher}} will produce the following result. {{tj|cite book |last: Author's last name|first: Author's first name |author-link: Author's page name on Wikipedia|date: Publication date |title: Work's title|url: Work's URL|location: Where the work was published |publisher: Name of the publisher}} Likewise, the following code {{tj|Infobox movie quote|name:|image:|caption:|character:|actor:|writer: |firstusedin:|alsousedin:|moviequotes:}} will produce the following result. {{tj|Infobox movie quote|name:|image:|caption:|character:|actor:|writer: |firstusedin:|alsousedin:|moviequotes:}} And finally, the following code {{tj|Blockquote | Cry "Havoc" and let slip the dogs of war. | [[William Shakespeare]] | character: Mark Antony | title: ''[[Julius Caesar (play)|Julius Caesar]]'' | source: act III, scene I }} will produce the following result. {{tj|Blockquote | Cry "Havoc" and let slip the dogs of war. | [[William Shakespeare]] | character: Mark Antony | title: ''[[Julius Caesar (play)|Julius Caesar]]'' | source: act III, scene I }} === Edge cases === To preserve one or more initial colons from being replaced, the : HTML entity must be used. After one colon has been replaced all other colons in the argument will be preserved. Similarly, to insert one or more equals signs into a parameter content, either {{=}} or = must be used. Thus, the following are all valid parameters, {{tj|Some template | Hello | World | foo: bar | | road: | keep:the:colon:I:said:keep:it! | ink :marble | book: /books?id{{=}}mAlLt1aGsWYC }} and they will produce the following result. {{tj|Some template | Hello | World | foo: bar | | road: | keep:the:colon:I:said:keep:it! | ink :marble | book: /books?id{{=}}mAlLt1aGsWYC }} Arguments containing one or more unescaped equals signs will be silently ignored. Thus, the following code {{tj|Some template | Hello | this = will = be = ignored | road: unknown }} will produce the following problematic result. {{tj|Some template | Hello | this = will = be = ignored | road: unknown }} Instead, the right way to write the previous unlikely example is the following, {{tj|Some template | Hello | this {{=}} will {{=}} not {{=}} be {{=}} ignored | road: unknown }} which will correctly print {{tj|Some template | Hello | this {{=}} will {{=}} not {{=}} be {{=}} ignored | road: unknown }} By design, there is no way to deviate from the format of one argument per line (vertical format). If you need to display a template and all its arguments in one single line (horizontal format), please refer to {{tl|tji}}. If you need a more irregular output, please refer to other solutions. == Before editing this template == * Do not add optional arguments for offering customized results (e.g. changing colors on demand, font, etc.): one of the purposes of this template is that of maintaining always the same look across the entire Wikipedia concerning how templates are shown and explained * If you intend to introduce substantial changes that are not simple bug fixes, you are invited to discuss them in the [[Template talk:Template journal|talk page]] first * If you do make changes, please make always sure that the {{tl|tji}} template remains consistent with this template == Subpages == * {{Template journal/arg}} – no longer used, will be removed at some point * {{rel|Template:Template journal/styles.css}} == See also == * {{Tl|Template journal inline}} * {{Tl|Template journal parameter}} * {{Tl|Template link}} * {{Tl|Template link expanded}} * {{Tlx|Syntaxhighlight|lang{{=}}wikitext}} ** {{xtag|syntaxhighlight|p|params=lang="wikitext"}} {{semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Wikitext typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Internal template-link templates]] [[Category:Variadic templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Template shortcut|tj|TJ}} {{lua|Module:Params}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Template journal/styles.css}} The {{tl|Template journal}} template allows to showcase with colors and multiple lines the syntax of any template, while providing an easy way to display placeholder texts using colons as separators. For an identical template that displays the output in horizontal format, see {{tl|Template journal inline}}. See also {{tl|Template journal parameter}} for a template similar to {{tl|para}} that uses the same syntax and produces the same output as the parameters displayed by {{tl|tj}} and {{tl|tji}}. == Usage == ;Syntax :{{tji|tj|template|param. name 1: param. content 1|param. name 2: param. content 2...}} {{vpad}} For instance, the following code {{tj|cite book |last: Author's last name|first: Author's first name |author-link: Author's page name on Wikipedia|date: Publication date |title: Work's title|url: Work's URL|location: Where the work was published |publisher: Name of the publisher}} will produce the following result. {{tj|cite book |last: Author's last name|first: Author's first name |author-link: Author's page name on Wikipedia|date: Publication date |title: Work's title|url: Work's URL|location: Where the work was published |publisher: Name of the publisher}} Likewise, the following code {{tj|Infobox movie quote|name:|image:|caption:|character:|actor:|writer: |firstusedin:|alsousedin:|moviequotes:}} will produce the following result. {{tj|Infobox movie quote|name:|image:|caption:|character:|actor:|writer: |firstusedin:|alsousedin:|moviequotes:}} And finally, the following code {{tj|Blockquote | Cry "Havoc" and let slip the dogs of war. | [[William Shakespeare]] | character: Mark Antony | title: ''[[Julius Caesar (play)|Julius Caesar]]'' | source: act III, scene I }} will produce the following result. {{tj|Blockquote | Cry "Havoc" and let slip the dogs of war. | [[William Shakespeare]] | character: Mark Antony | title: ''[[Julius Caesar (play)|Julius Caesar]]'' | source: act III, scene I }} === Edge cases === To preserve one or more initial colons from being replaced, the : HTML entity must be used. After one colon has been replaced all other colons in the argument will be preserved. Similarly, to insert one or more equals signs into a parameter content, either {{=}} or = must be used. Thus, the following are all valid parameters, {{tj|Some template | Hello | World | foo: bar | | road: | keep:the:colon:I:said:keep:it! | ink :marble | book: /books?id{{=}}mAlLt1aGsWYC }} and they will produce the following result. {{tj|Some template | Hello | World | foo: bar | | road: | keep:the:colon:I:said:keep:it! | ink :marble | book: /books?id{{=}}mAlLt1aGsWYC }} Arguments containing one or more unescaped equals signs will be silently ignored. Thus, the following code {{tj|Some template | Hello | this = will = be = ignored | road: unknown }} will produce the following problematic result. {{tj|Some template | Hello | this = will = be = ignored | road: unknown }} Instead, the right way to write the previous unlikely example is the following, {{tj|Some template | Hello | this {{=}} will {{=}} not {{=}} be {{=}} ignored | road: unknown }} which will correctly print {{tj|Some template | Hello | this {{=}} will {{=}} not {{=}} be {{=}} ignored | road: unknown }} By design, there is no way to deviate from the format of one argument per line (vertical format). If you need to display a template and all its arguments in one single line (horizontal format), please refer to {{tl|tji}}. If you need a more irregular output, please refer to other solutions. == Before editing this template == * Do not add optional arguments for offering customized results (e.g. changing colors on demand, font, etc.): one of the purposes of this template is that of maintaining always the same look across the entire Wikipedia concerning how templates are shown and explained * If you intend to introduce substantial changes that are not simple bug fixes, you are invited to discuss them in the [[Template talk:Template journal|talk page]] first * If you do make changes, please make always sure that the {{tl|tji}} template remains consistent with this template == Subpages == * {{Template journal/arg}} – no longer used, will be removed at some point * {{rel|Template:Template journal/styles.css}} == See also == * {{Tl|Template journal inline}} * {{Tl|Template journal parameter}} * {{Tl|Template link}} * {{Tl|Template link expanded}} * {{Tlx|Syntaxhighlight|lang{{=}}wikitext}} ** {{xtag|syntaxhighlight|p|params=lang="wikitext"}} {{semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Wikitext typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Internal template-link templates]] [[Category:Variadic templates]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Template shortcut|tj|TJ}} {{lua|Module:Params}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Template journal/styles.css}} The {{tl|Template journal}} template allows to showcase with colors and multiple lines the syntax of any template, while providing an easy way to display placeholder texts using colons as separators. For an identical template that displays the output in horizontal format, see {{tl|Template journal inline}}. See also {{tl|Template journal parameter}} for a template similar to {{tl|para}} that uses the same syntax and produces the same output as the parameters displayed by {{tl|tj}} and {{tl|tji}}. == Usage == ;Syntax :{{tji|tj|template|param. name 1: param. content 1|param. name 2: param. content 2...}} {{vpad}} For instance, the following code {{tj|cite book |last: Author's last name|first: Author's first name |author-link: Author's page name on Wikipedia|date: Publication date |title: Work's title|url: Work's URL|location: Where the work was published |publisher: Name of the publisher}} will produce the following result. {{tj|cite book |last: Author's last name|first: Author's first name |author-link: Author's page name on Wikipedia|date: Publication date |title: Work's title|url: Work's URL|location: Where the work was published |publisher: Name of the publisher}} Likewise, the following code {{tj|Infobox movie quote|name:|image:|caption:|character:|actor:|writer: |firstusedin:|alsousedin:|moviequotes:}} will produce the following result. {{tj|Infobox movie quote|name:|image:|caption:|character:|actor:|writer: |firstusedin:|alsousedin:|moviequotes:}} And finally, the following code {{tj|Blockquote | Cry "Havoc" and let slip the dogs of war. | [[William Shakespeare]] | character: Mark Antony | title: ''[[Julius Caesar (play)|Julius Caesar]]'' | source: act III, scene I }} will produce the following result. {{tj|Blockquote | Cry "Havoc" and let slip the dogs of war. | [[William Shakespeare]] | character: Mark Antony | title: ''[[Julius Caesar (play)|Julius Caesar]]'' | source: act III, scene I }} === Edge cases === To preserve one or more initial colons from being replaced, the : HTML entity must be used. After one colon has been replaced all other colons in the argument will be preserved. Similarly, to insert one or more equals signs into a parameter content, either {{=}} or = must be used. Thus, the following are all valid parameters, {{tj|Some template | Hello | World | foo: bar | | road: | keep:the:colon:I:said:keep:it! | ink :marble | book: /books?id{{=}}mAlLt1aGsWYC }} and they will produce the following result. {{tj|Some template | Hello | World | foo: bar | | road: | keep:the:colon:I:said:keep:it! | ink :marble | book: /books?id{{=}}mAlLt1aGsWYC }} Arguments containing one or more unescaped equals signs will be silently ignored. Thus, the following code {{tj|Some template | Hello | this = will = be = ignored | road: unknown }} will produce the following problematic result. {{tj|Some template | Hello | this = will = be = ignored | road: unknown }} Instead, the right way to write the previous unlikely example is the following, {{tj|Some template | Hello | this {{=}} will {{=}} not {{=}} be {{=}} ignored | road: unknown }} which will correctly print {{tj|Some template | Hello | this {{=}} will {{=}} not {{=}} be {{=}} ignored | road: unknown }} By design, there is no way to deviate from the format of one argument per line (vertical format). If you need to display a template and all its arguments in one single line (horizontal format), please refer to {{tl|tji}}. If you need a more irregular output, please refer to other solutions. == Before editing this template == * Do not add optional arguments for offering customized results (e.g. changing colors on demand, font, etc.): one of the purposes of this template is that of maintaining always the same look across the entire Wikipedia concerning how templates are shown and explained * If you intend to introduce substantial changes that are not simple bug fixes, you are invited to discuss them in the [[Template talk:Template journal|talk page]] first * If you do make changes, please make always sure that the {{tl|tji}} template remains consistent with this template == Subpages == * {{Template journal/arg}} – no longer used, will be removed at some point * {{rel|Template:Template journal/styles.css}} == See also == * {{Tl|Template journal inline}} * {{Tl|Template journal parameter}} * {{Tl|Template link}} * {{Tl|Template link expanded}} * {{Tlx|Syntaxhighlight|lang{{=}}wikitext}} ** {{xtag|syntaxhighlight|p|params=lang="wikitext"}} {{semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Wikitext typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Internal template-link templates]] [[Category:Variadic templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Template shortcut|tj|TJ}} {{lua|Module:Params}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Template journal/styles.css}} The {{tl|Template journal}} template allows to showcase with colors and multiple lines the syntax of any template, while providing an easy way to display placeholder texts using colons as separators. For an identical template that displays the output in horizontal format, see {{tl|Template journal inline}}. See also {{tl|Template journal parameter}} for a template similar to {{tl|para}} that uses the same syntax and produces the same output as the parameters displayed by {{tl|tj}} and {{tl|tji}}. == Usage == ;Syntax :{{tji|tj|template|param. name 1: param. content 1|param. name 2: param. content 2...}} {{vpad}} For instance, the following code {{tj|cite book |last: Author's last name|first: Author's first name |author-link: Author's page name on Wikipedia|date: Publication date |title: Work's title|url: Work's URL|location: Where the work was published |publisher: Name of the publisher}} will produce the following result. {{tj|cite book |last: Author's last name|first: Author's first name |author-link: Author's page name on Wikipedia|date: Publication date |title: Work's title|url: Work's URL|location: Where the work was published |publisher: Name of the publisher}} Likewise, the following code {{tj|Infobox movie quote|name:|image:|caption:|character:|actor:|writer: |firstusedin:|alsousedin:|moviequotes:}} will produce the following result. {{tj|Infobox movie quote|name:|image:|caption:|character:|actor:|writer: |firstusedin:|alsousedin:|moviequotes:}} And finally, the following code {{tj|Blockquote | Cry "Havoc" and let slip the dogs of war. | [[William Shakespeare]] | character: Mark Antony | title: ''[[Julius Caesar (play)|Julius Caesar]]'' | source: act III, scene I }} will produce the following result. {{tj|Blockquote | Cry "Havoc" and let slip the dogs of war. | [[William Shakespeare]] | character: Mark Antony | title: ''[[Julius Caesar (play)|Julius Caesar]]'' | source: act III, scene I }} === Edge cases === To preserve one or more initial colons from being replaced, the : HTML entity must be used. After one colon has been replaced all other colons in the argument will be preserved. Similarly, to insert one or more equals signs into a parameter content, either {{=}} or = must be used. Thus, the following are all valid parameters, {{tj|Some template | Hello | World | foo: bar | | road: | keep:the:colon:I:said:keep:it! | ink :marble | book: /books?id{{=}}mAlLt1aGsWYC }} and they will produce the following result. {{tj|Some template | Hello | World | foo: bar | | road: | keep:the:colon:I:said:keep:it! | ink :marble | book: /books?id{{=}}mAlLt1aGsWYC }} Arguments containing one or more unescaped equals signs will be silently ignored. Thus, the following code {{tj|Some template | Hello | this = will = be = ignored | road: unknown }} will produce the following problematic result. {{tj|Some template | Hello | this = will = be = ignored | road: unknown }} Instead, the right way to write the previous unlikely example is the following, {{tj|Some template | Hello | this {{=}} will {{=}} not {{=}} be {{=}} ignored | road: unknown }} which will correctly print {{tj|Some template | Hello | this {{=}} will {{=}} not {{=}} be {{=}} ignored | road: unknown }} By design, there is no way to deviate from the format of one argument per line (vertical format). If you need to display a template and all its arguments in one single line (horizontal format), please refer to {{tl|tji}}. If you need a more irregular output, please refer to other solutions. == Before editing this template == * Do not add optional arguments for offering customized results (e.g. changing colors on demand, font, etc.): one of the purposes of this template is that of maintaining always the same look across the entire Wikipedia concerning how templates are shown and explained * If you intend to introduce substantial changes that are not simple bug fixes, you are invited to discuss them in the [[Template talk:Template journal|talk page]] first * If you do make changes, please make always sure that the {{tl|tji}} template remains consistent with this template == Subpages == * {{Template journal/arg}} – no longer used, will be removed at some point * {{rel|Template:Template journal/styles.css}} == See also == * {{Tl|Template journal inline}} * {{Tl|Template journal parameter}} * {{Tl|Template link}} * {{Tl|Template link expanded}} * {{Tlx|Syntaxhighlight|lang{{=}}wikitext}} ** {{xtag|syntaxhighlight|p|params=lang="wikitext"}} {{semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Wikitext typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Internal template-link templates]] [[Category:Variadic templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 4 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23626449376|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Template shortcut|tj|TJ}} {{lua|Module:Params}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Template journal/styles.css}} The {{tl|Template journal}} template allows to showcase with colors and multiple lines the syntax of any template, while providing an easy way to display placeholder texts using colons as separators. For an identical template that displays the output in horizontal format, see {{tl|Template journal inline}}. See also {{tl|Template journal parameter}} for a template similar to {{tl|para}} that uses the same syntax and produces the same output as the parameters displayed by {{tl|tj}} and {{tl|tji}}. == Usage == ;Syntax :{{tji|tj|template|param. name 1: param. content 1|param. name 2: param. content 2...}} {{vpad}} For instance, the following code {{tj|cite book |last: Author's last name|first: Author's first name |author-link: Author's page name on Wikipedia|date: Publication date |title: Work's title|url: Work's URL|location: Where the work was published |publisher: Name of the publisher}} will produce the following result. {{tj|cite book |last: Author's last name|first: Author's first name |author-link: Author's page name on Wikipedia|date: Publication date |title: Work's title|url: Work's URL|location: Where the work was published |publisher: Name of the publisher}} Likewise, the following code {{tj|Infobox movie quote|name:|image:|caption:|character:|actor:|writer: |firstusedin:|alsousedin:|moviequotes:}} will produce the following result. {{tj|Infobox movie quote|name:|image:|caption:|character:|actor:|writer: |firstusedin:|alsousedin:|moviequotes:}} And finally, the following code {{tj|Blockquote | Cry "Havoc" and let slip the dogs of war. | [[William Shakespeare]] | character: Mark Antony | title: ''[[Julius Caesar (play)|Julius Caesar]]'' | source: act III, scene I }} will produce the following result. {{tj|Blockquote | Cry "Havoc" and let slip the dogs of war. | [[William Shakespeare]] | character: Mark Antony | title: ''[[Julius Caesar (play)|Julius Caesar]]'' | source: act III, scene I }} === Edge cases === To preserve one or more initial colons from being replaced, the : HTML entity must be used. After one colon has been replaced all other colons in the argument will be preserved. Similarly, to insert one or more equals signs into a parameter content, either {{=}} or = must be used. Thus, the following are all valid parameters, {{tj|Some template | Hello | World | foo: bar | | road: | keep:the:colon:I:said:keep:it! | ink :marble | book: /books?id{{=}}mAlLt1aGsWYC }} and they will produce the following result. {{tj|Some template | Hello | World | foo: bar | | road: | keep:the:colon:I:said:keep:it! | ink :marble | book: /books?id{{=}}mAlLt1aGsWYC }} Arguments containing one or more unescaped equals signs will be silently ignored. Thus, the following code {{tj|Some template | Hello | this = will = be = ignored | road: unknown }} will produce the following problematic result. {{tj|Some template | Hello | this = will = be = ignored | road: unknown }} Instead, the right way to write the previous unlikely example is the following, {{tj|Some template | Hello | this {{=}} will {{=}} not {{=}} be {{=}} ignored | road: unknown }} which will correctly print {{tj|Some template | Hello | this {{=}} will {{=}} not {{=}} be {{=}} ignored | road: unknown }} By design, there is no way to deviate from the format of one argument per line (vertical format). If you need to display a template and all its arguments in one single line (horizontal format), please refer to {{tl|tji}}. If you need a more irregular output, please refer to other solutions. == Before editing this template == * Do not add optional arguments for offering customized results (e.g. changing colors on demand, font, etc.): one of the purposes of this template is that of maintaining always the same look across the entire Wikipedia concerning how templates are shown and explained * If you intend to introduce substantial changes that are not simple bug fixes, you are invited to discuss them in the [[Template talk:Template journal|talk page]] first * If you do make changes, please make always sure that the {{tl|tji}} template remains consistent with this template == Subpages == * {{Template journal/arg}} – no longer used, will be removed at some point * {{rel|Template:Template journal/styles.css}} == See also == * {{Tl|Template journal inline}} * {{Tl|Template journal parameter}} * {{Tl|Template link}} * {{Tl|Template link expanded}} * {{Tlx|Syntaxhighlight|lang{{=}}wikitext}} ** {{xtag|syntaxhighlight|p|params=lang="wikitext"}} {{semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Wikitext typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Internal template-link templates]] [[Category:Variadic templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24984760715|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Template shortcut|tj|TJ}} {{lua|Module:Params}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Template journal/styles.css}} The {{tl|Template journal}} template allows to showcase with colors and multiple lines the syntax of any template, while providing an easy way to display placeholder texts using colons as separators. For an identical template that displays the output in horizontal format, see {{tl|Template journal inline}}. See also {{tl|Template journal parameter}} for a template similar to {{tl|para}} that uses the same syntax and produces the same output as the parameters displayed by {{tl|tj}} and {{tl|tji}}. == Usage == ;Syntax :{{tji|tj|template|param. name 1: param. content 1|param. name 2: param. content 2...}} {{vpad}} For instance, the following code {{tj|cite book |last: Author's last name|first: Author's first name |author-link: Author's page name on Wikipedia|date: Publication date |title: Work's title|url: Work's URL|location: Where the work was published |publisher: Name of the publisher}} will produce the following result. {{tj|cite book |last: Author's last name|first: Author's first name |author-link: Author's page name on Wikipedia|date: Publication date |title: Work's title|url: Work's URL|location: Where the work was published |publisher: Name of the publisher}} Likewise, the following code {{tj|Infobox movie quote|name:|image:|caption:|character:|actor:|writer: |firstusedin:|alsousedin:|moviequotes:}} will produce the following result. {{tj|Infobox movie quote|name:|image:|caption:|character:|actor:|writer: |firstusedin:|alsousedin:|moviequotes:}} And finally, the following code {{tj|Blockquote | Cry "Havoc" and let slip the dogs of war. | [[William Shakespeare]] | character: Mark Antony | title: ''[[Julius Caesar (play)|Julius Caesar]]'' | source: act III, scene I }} will produce the following result. {{tj|Blockquote | Cry "Havoc" and let slip the dogs of war. | [[William Shakespeare]] | character: Mark Antony | title: ''[[Julius Caesar (play)|Julius Caesar]]'' | source: act III, scene I }} === Edge cases === To preserve one or more initial colons from being replaced, the : HTML entity must be used. After one colon has been replaced all other colons in the argument will be preserved. Similarly, to insert one or more equals signs into a parameter content, either {{=}} or = must be used. Thus, the following are all valid parameters, {{tj|Some template | Hello | World | foo: bar | | road: | keep:the:colon:I:said:keep:it! | ink :marble | book: /books?id{{=}}mAlLt1aGsWYC }} and they will produce the following result. {{tj|Some template | Hello | World | foo: bar | | road: | keep:the:colon:I:said:keep:it! | ink :marble | book: /books?id{{=}}mAlLt1aGsWYC }} Arguments containing one or more unescaped equals signs will be silently ignored. Thus, the following code {{tj|Some template | Hello | this = will = be = ignored | road: unknown }} will produce the following problematic result. {{tj|Some template | Hello | this = will = be = ignored | road: unknown }} Instead, the right way to write the previous unlikely example is the following, {{tj|Some template | Hello | this {{=}} will {{=}} not {{=}} be {{=}} ignored | road: unknown }} which will correctly print {{tj|Some template | Hello | this {{=}} will {{=}} not {{=}} be {{=}} ignored | road: unknown }} By design, there is no way to deviate from the format of one argument per line (vertical format). If you need to display a template and all its arguments in one single line (horizontal format), please refer to {{tl|tji}}. If you need a more irregular output, please refer to other solutions. == Before editing this template == * Do not add optional arguments for offering customized results (e.g. changing colors on demand, font, etc.): one of the purposes of this template is that of maintaining always the same look across the entire Wikipedia concerning how templates are shown and explained * If you intend to introduce substantial changes that are not simple bug fixes, you are invited to discuss them in the [[Template talk:Template journal|talk page]] first * If you do make changes, please make always sure that the {{tl|tji}} template remains consistent with this template == Subpages == * {{Template journal/arg}} – no longer used, will be removed at some point * {{rel|Template:Template journal/styles.css}} == See also == * {{Tl|Template journal inline}} * {{Tl|Template journal parameter}} * {{Tl|Template link}} * {{Tl|Template link expanded}} * {{Tlx|Syntaxhighlight|lang{{=}}wikitext}} ** {{xtag|syntaxhighlight|p|params=lang="wikitext"}} {{semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Wikitext typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Internal template-link templates]] [[Category:Variadic templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
